metadata.title: DixieData PDF Export Field Requirements Survey
metadata.description: Help us decide which data fields belong in each type of PDF export produced by DixieData. The application currently generates 6 distinct PDF types. Your answers will shape which fields appear, in what order, and which sections are visible. Estimated time: 15–25 minutes. There are no wrong answers — if a field does not apply to a particular PDF type, choose 'Not applicable' or skip the row.

intro_questions.title: How would you describe your relationship to the data?
How familiar are you with the current DixieData PDF exports?
intro_questions.scale_max_label: I have produced every export type

intro_questions.title: Which PDF export types have you used or expect to use? (Select all that apply)

pdf_export_sections.section_title: 1. Single Record PDF (Soldier / Widow / Wife / Person Record)
pdf_export_sections.section_description: Generated from the soldier detail page. One person per export. Available in landscape (full biography on page 2) or portrait (compact, with biography excerpt). May include images or printer-friendly (no images).

questions.title: Section A — Title Block (top of page 1)
questions.help: The header that identifies the record. Already includes display ID and entry type label (Soldier / Widow / Wife / Person Record).

questions.title: Which title-block fields should the Single Record PDF always show?
columns: Person's full name (prefix + first + middle + last + suffix)
Entry type label (Soldier / Widow / Wife / Person Record)

questions.title: Section B — Identity & Vital Details
Which Identity & Vital Details fields should appear?
columns: Birth Info (free-text fallback when birth date unknown)

questions.title: Section C — Service & Archive Details
Which Service & Archive Details fields should appear?
Section D — Household & Context
Which Household & Context fields should appear?
columns: Linked Spouse Record (cross-reference to spouse's own record)

questions.title: Section E — Records (linked child records)
Which sub-fields of each linked record should appear?
columns: App ID (per child row, in parentheses)

questions.title: Section F — Images
Should images appear in the Single Record PDF by default?
options: Off by default; user opts in

questions.title: Captions are currently suppressed in the printable layout. Should captions appear?
options: Yes — show captions under each image
Show only the primary image's caption

questions.title: Section G — Biography
How should the biography appear in the Single Record PDF?
options: Inline portrait layout (excerpt only, on page 1)
Full biography on dedicated page 2 (landscape default)
Always full biography, regardless of orientation
Always excerpt only, no full biography page

questions.title: Field note: The PDF currently prefers `pdf_excerpt_override` if present, else falls back to `biography`. Should this behavior change?
When both the excerpt override and full biography exist, which should the PDF show?
options: Show excerpt override AND full biography
Let the user choose at export time

questions.title: Anything else that should appear in the Single Record PDF that we did not list?

pdf_export_sections.section_description: Generated from the Share page. One record per page, landscape by default, configurable scope (all / filtered / manually selected), with optional sorting and grouping. Essentially the Single Record card repeated for every person in scope.

questions.title: Should the Bulk Printable PDF show exactly the same fields per record as the Single Record PDF?
options: Yes — mirror the Single Record PDF exactly
No — let users configure Bulk fields independently (show fewer fields per page)

questions.title: If fewer fields per page, which fields are essential in the Bulk Printable PDF? (Select all that apply)
options: Entry type label (Soldier / Widow / Wife / Person Record)

questions.title: Section — Grouping (optional group divider pages between sections)
Which grouping axes should the Bulk PDF allow? (Select all that apply)
options: Entry Type (Soldier / Widow / Wife / Person Record)

questions.title: Which sorting orders should be available?
options: By Pension State then last name

questions.title: Should the Bulk PDF ever include images?
How should biographies appear in the Bulk PDF?
options: Excerpt only (compact, one page per person)
Full biography on page 2 (mirror Single Record landscape behavior)
User opt-in via Full Biography Page toggle (current behavior)

questions.title: Anything else for the Bulk Printable PDF?

pdf_export_sections.section_description: Generated from the Calendar page. One calendar month per export, listing every person whose birth or death anniversary falls on each day.

questions.title: Which fields should appear next to each person's name on an anniversary day?
columns: Entry type label (Soldier / Widow / Wife / Person Record)
Birth year (so the user can tell the century)
Death year (so the user can tell the century)

questions.title: How should the anniversary list be organized?
options: Separate Birth Anniversaries and Death Anniversaries sections per day
Separate sections per entry type within each day
Single chronological list with date headers

questions.title: Should the anniversary PDF allow filtering by year range (e.g. only 19th-century anniversaries)? If yes, describe the default range.
Anything else for the Anniversary PDF?

pdf_export_sections.section_description: Generated from the Insights page. Aggregate statistics about the whole archive — record counts, top cemeteries, Confederate Home participation, pension distribution, unit representation, and chronological overview.

questions.title: The 'Record Types' section currently shows Soldiers / Spouses (Wives & Widows) / Linked People counts. Should these be split?
options: Split Spouses into separate Wife and Widow counts
Split Soldiers into Officers vs. Enlisted
Add a 'Total records (soldiers + all linked persons)' headline

questions.title: Several sections show 'top N' lists. What N should each use?
Top Cemeteries — how many entries?
Most frequent Confederate Home names — how many entries?
Unit Representation — how many entries?
Pension Distribution — show every state, or only states with members?
options: Every state with at least one record (current)
Only states with more than N members (configurable)

questions.title: Chronological Overview — birth and death decades. Which granularity?
Which additional analytics sections (if any) should the Archive Summary PDF include?
options: Records per Unit breakdown (already implied by Unit Representation but expanded)
Records per Confederate Home expanded view
Records per Burial Location expanded view
Average biography length per entry type

questions.title: Anything else for the Analytics Summary PDF?

pdf_export_sections.section_description: A dedicated full-biography page that can be appended to a Single Record PDF or Bulk Printable PDF. One biography per page.
pdf_export_sections.ui_button_label: Not currently exposed in UI (template exists)

questions.title: Which contextual fields should appear at the top of each biography page?
Should the Biography Appendix be appended by default when biographies exist?
options: Yes — append when a biography is non-empty

questions.title: Anything else for the Biography Appendix?

pdf_export_sections.section_title: 6. Global Preferences (apply to every PDF type)
pdf_export_sections.section_description: Cross-cutting concerns: privacy, metadata, footer, ordering.

questions.title: What should appear in the page footer of every PDF?
options: Display ID of the current record (on Bulk PDF pages)

questions.title: On the Single Record PDF, rank the order of these sections (top = first visible). Drag to reorder, or 1 = first, 2 = second, etc.
Are there any fields that should NEVER appear in a public-facing PDF? (Select all that apply)
options: None — current privacy model is sufficient

questions.title: The DB also contains these fields that are NOT currently rendered in any PDF. Should any of them appear in future exports? (List field names and which PDF type)
questions.help: Currently unmapped: rank (single), maiden_name on soldier entry_type, relationship_label, added_by, last_edited_by, last_edited_fields, last_edited_at, import_batch_id, sync_id, scratchpad_cache.

questions.title: Final question
If we can only fix or add ONE field behavior in the next PDF release, what should it be?

notes: Each top-level key under pdf_export_sections becomes a Form Section (Page Break before it).
Each `questions` array becomes the section's items, in order.
Question type mappings to Google Forms ItemType:
CHECKBOX_GRID -> use CHECKBOX with each column as an option, prefixed with the row label (Include/Exclude)
RANKING       -> use MULTIPLE_CHOICE with ranked options, or write a custom Apps Script ranking widget
Title block of each Form section should be section_title. section_description goes in the section description field.
Required = true maps to RequiredQuestion; required = false leaves it optional.
Help text in `help` key goes in the question description.
